channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: Freed Gaza flotilla activists allege Israeli abuse including rape
item.description: Activists released from Israeli custody after being detained on a flotilla trying to bring aid to Gaza were subjected to abuse, organisers said on Friday, with several hospitalised with injuries and at least 15 reporting sexual assaults, including rape.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard resigns as Trump’s national intelligence director
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard resigned as President Donald Trump’s director of national intelligence on Friday, saying she needed to step away as her husband battles cancer. She is the fourth Cabinet official to depart during Trump’s second term.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Turkey: Thousands rally after court ousts Turkish opposition leader
item.description: Thousands rallied on Friday in Ankara and Istanbul to protest against a court verdict ousting the popular leader of Turkey's main opposition CHP party in the latest government crackdown on foes.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Ebola: Highest risk level in DR Congo warns World Health Organization
item.description: Emmet Livingstone has more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Mexico, EU sign stalled trade deal as they aim to diversify from US
item.description: Mexico and the European Union signed a long-stalled free trade agreement on Friday as they seek to decrease dependence on the U.S. and partially insulate themselves from ​U.S. President Donald ‌Trump's tariffs.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Pelé’s third, Baggio’s miss and the ‘Hand of God’: Iconic World Cups in Mexico and the US
item.description: From Pelé’s third title to Maradona’s legendary double and Baggio’s agonising penalty miss, tournaments hosted in Mexico and the US have left us with some of the most memorable moments in World Cup history. Here’s a throwback to the 1970, 1986 and 1994 editions.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin DODMAN

item.title: Cuba: Castro backers rally in front of United States embassy in Havana
item.description: Thousands of protesters waving Cuban flags rallied in front of the US embassy in Havana on Friday, in a show of support for ex-president Raul Castro following his US indictment.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Experts urge authorities to limit number of climbers on Mount Everest
item.description: Experts urge authorities to limit number of climbers on Mount Everest due to a overcrowing issue.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Cannes Film Festival: Barbra Streisand to receive Palme d'Or
item.description: The 79th Cannes Film Festival draws to a close Saturday with the presentation of one of cinema’s highest honors, the Palme d’Or.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: War in the Middle East: US-Iran talks make headway
item.description: Anahita Motazed Rad, Associate Fellow at the London School of Economics, is our guest.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Activists allege Israeli sexual assault: Israeli officials deny accusations
item.description: Adrien Berthel, Activist at Global Sumud France, is our guest.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: France: Segement of Eiffel Tower stairs dating back to 1889 sold
item.description: Three metre section of the Eiffel Tower dating back to 1889 has been sold.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Senegal's President Faye fires prime minister after years of tensions
item.description: Senegal’s President Bassirou Diomaye Faye has fired Prime Minister Ousmane Sonko after years of simmering tension.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Tens of thousands ordered to evacuate in California over leaking chemical tank
item.description: Authorities in southern California ordered the evacuation of 40,000 people on Friday after a huge storage tank began leaking a hazardous chemical used to make plastic parts, posing the risk of an explosion.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Eva Longoria: « My greatest success is still in front of me »
item.description:
🎞️ For the 21st time, one of the world’s most celebrated actresses and filmmakers returns to the red carpet at the Cannes Film Festival.
☕📽️ Eva Longoria opens up about what she wishes she had known before her very first Cannes experience, shares some of her most memorable moments, reflects on her passion for cinema and filmmaking and reveals what’s next on the horizon✨
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: 🔴 Senegal's President Bassirou Diomaye Faye fires prime minister after years of tensions
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: China coal mine explosion kills at least 90 people, more missing
item.description: A gas explosion at a coal mine in China’s northern Shanxi province killed at least 90 people and trapped dozens underground, state media reported Saturday, in the country's biggest mining disaster in more than 15 years.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Middle East war live: Iran considers latest peace proposal, accuses US of 'excessive demands'
item.description: Tehran accused the United States of "excessive demands", Iranian media reported on Saturday, as US media reports suggested that Washington was mulling new strikes while Iran was considering the latest peace proposal. Read our liveblog for all the latest developments.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Rubio begins India visit to bolser ties after Trump's China summit
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio began a high-profile visit to India on Saturday for talks with Prime Minister Narendra Modi aimed at reinforcing ties days after President Donald Trump’s closely watched summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Who will win the Palme d'Or? Cannes highlights as festival wraps up
item.description: The Cannes Film Festival wraps up Saturday with its traditional awards ceremony after two action-packed weeks of world premieres, star-studded red carpets, parties and fashion shows. Here's a look back on some of the festival's highlights.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Senegal's president fires PM Sonko, dissolves government
item.description: Senegal President Bassirou Diomaye Faye on Friday dismissed Prime Minister Ousmane Sonko and dissolved the government after months of mounting tensions between the two leaders, plunging the debt-stricken West African nation deeper into political uncertainty.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Hope can help you 'find your way through the fog', Angélique Kidjo unveils new album
item.description: Angélique Kidjo is a global music icon, five-time Grammy winner, and unstoppable force for change. The Beninese superstar has just released her powerful new album HOPE!!, a groove-heavy, galvanizing work dedicated to her late mother, featuring collaborations with Pharrell Williams, Ayra Starr, Davido, and more. Fresh from her Hollywood Walk of Fame star and ongoing world tour, Kidjo continues to blend African rhythms with universal messages of resilience and unity. Jean-Emile Jammine speaks to a living legend and passionate activist.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Rubio urges more support from NATO allies to help end Iran war
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio urged more support for US efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, relaying Trump's "disappointment" at NATO allies and European partners for failing to step up their efforts to end the Iran war. Rubio made the comments ahead of the arrival of Pakistan’s army chief in Tehran on Friday to advance mediation between Iran and Washington.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Bolivian President Paz targeted by new mass protests
item.description: Bolivian riot police fired tear gas at anti-government demonstrators in La Paz on Friday as unions and Indigenous groups renewed demands for President Rodrigo Paz to resign amid a deepening economic crisis and mounting unrest.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24
